---
title: "SMSN Quarto workshop template and materials"
author: Tom Coleman
format: html
---

[Download source files from the GitHub repository at this link](https://github.com/tdhc153/smsn-quarto-workshop)

[Download the book template from the GitHub repository at this link](https://github.com/tdhc153/smsn-quarto-workshop-book)

[Quarto resource template](resourcetemplate.qmd)

[Quarto slides template](slidestemplate.qmd)

[Slides used in the workshop](masterslidedeck.qmd)

Quarto is a powerful open-source typesetting language. Boasting both R and Python functionality, it can be used to write a huge range of outputs including short-form html documents, interactive Python or R workbooks via Jupyter, dynamic accessible slides, or even traditional lecture notes.

This hands-on interactive workshop will teach you how to create and build Quarto documents. The workshop will cover: how to build Quarto documents, writing content in Quarto, working with revealjs slides, and working with longer-form documents such as lecture notes. Templates will be provided for ease of use. 

To participate in this workshop, you will need to download either RStudio to your computer, or to use Posit Cloud (a free cloud-based service to run RStudio projects) during the session.

[Any problems? Please contact me at tdhc (at) st (dash) andrews (dot) ac (dot) uk.](mailto:tdhc@st-andrews.ac.uk)

[These materials are subject to a CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International license.](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/)

## After the session/using the resources

I would be very grateful if you took the time to fill in a Google form about the workshop/materials, in order to improve the workshop for potential future runnings and to help me understand the impact of today's workshop. All submissions are entirely anonymous.

![You can find the form at this QR code or at this link: <https://forms.gle/UeS478kobWTYbWDi9>](./figures/qr.png){width="50%" fig-align="center"}
